stories.title: Starmer Becomes First UK PM to Receive France's Légion d'Honneur
stories.summary: Emmanuel Macron awarded Keir Starmer France's highest honour, praising his 'decency' and leadership in supporting Ukraine. Starmer is the first UK Prime Minister to receive the Légion d'honneur.

stories.title: Ministers Salute Starmer's Long Service as He Prepares to Leave No. 10
stories.summary: David Lammy, the deputy prime minister, organised a ministerial send-off for the departing Labour leader. Starmer is set to hand over power to Andy Burnham next week.

stories.title: Andy Burnham Secures Labour Leadership with Landslide Support of MPs
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has won the Labour leadership contest with overwhelming parliamentary support, and is expected to enter Downing Street on Monday after the Commons begins its six-week summer recess.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: MPs Vote on Hillsborough Amendment — Starmer Opens Third Reading Debate
stories.summary: Keir Starmer opened the third reading debate on the Hillsborough law bill in the House of Commons. The government stated it intends to make 'broad use' of the duty of candour provision.

stories.title: Cyber Security and Resilience (Network and Information Systems) Bill Debated
stories.summary: The House of Commons held a debate on the Cyber Security and Resilience Bill. The bill covers network and information systems resilience for critical infrastructure.

stories.title: Tories Accuse Labour of 'Running Scared' Over Burnham's Parliamentary Appearance
stories.summary: The Conservatives demanded Andy Burnham face MPs before Parliament's summer recess, but ministers blocked the attempt. The Commons begins its six-week recess on Thursday.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: Andy Burnham Wins Labour Leadership with Landslide MP Support
stories.summary: Andy Burnham secured the Labour leadership with dominant parliamentary backing. The former Greater Manchester Mayor is set to become the next Prime Minister when Starmer formally steps down.

stories.title: Hillsborough Law Symbolises New Era as Power Passes from Starmer to Burnham
stories.summary: The Hillsborough law — personally championed by Andy Burnham — is passing through Parliament as the political torch is handed over. Burnham's long campaign for justice for Hillsborough victims has become a defining moment in the leadership transition.

stories.title: Public Asked: What Do You Want from the Next Labour Leader and Prime Minister?
stories.summary: Ahead of Andy Burnham taking over from Keir Starmer, The Guardian is gathering public views on what qualities and priorities people want to see in the next prime minister.

stories.title: Kemi Declares War on Net Zero — Purging Moderate Tory MPs
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch is intensifying her ideological shift in the Conservative Party, targeting MPs who accept climate science and pushing net zero sceptics. Moderates are being cast out as the party edges toward the right.

stories.title: Lord Barwell Loses Conservative Party Whip — Tories Deny Purge
stories.summary: Former minister Gavin Barwell lost the Conservative party whip, which he says suggests 'intolerance of criticism.' The party denied it was purging centrists, citing conduct issues.

stories.title: Tories Challenge Burnham to Face MPs Before Summer Recess
stories.summary: The Conservatives accused Labour of 'running scared' after ministers blocked a bid to have Andy Burnham appear before Parliament before the summer recess begins Thursday.

stories.title: Ed Davey Urges Burnham to Change Voting System Before Next Election
stories.summary: Lib Dem leader Sir Ed Davey called on incoming PM Andy Burnham to 'be brave' and fulfil his promise to reform the voting system. Davey said this would be essential before the next general election.

stories.title: Davey Joins BBC Discussion on Iran Tensions and Farage Security
stories.summary: Sir Ed Davey appeared on BBC Radio to discuss the US-Iran situation in the Strait of Hormuz as well as the ongoing security concerns raised following Ann Widdecombe's death.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe Killed in 'Targeted Attack', Police Say
stories.summary: Counter-terrorism police confirmed that former government minister and prominent Reform UK figure Ann Widdecombe was killed in a targeted attack. Police are still working to establish the motive.

stories.title: Government Accused of Failing Farage Over Security
stories.summary: Robert Jenrick, Reform's Treasury spokesperson, criticised the government for only arranging security talks for Nigel Farage 'as a result' of Ann Widdecombe's death. The BBC also reported the government is 'failing Farage over security.'

stories.title: Reform Accused of Trying to Criminalise Gaelic and Scots in Election Material
stories.summary: The SNP called a Reform UK amendment to the Representation of the People Bill 'despicable' and 'anti-Scottish', claiming it would ban the use of Gaelic and Scots language in election material.

stories.title: Yes Scotland Hands Accounts to Police After 'Missing £1.5m' Claim
stories.summary: Yes Scotland, which campaigned for independence in the 2014 referendum, handed accounts to police amid claims of missing funds. The group insists all funds have been accounted for, with the case linked to former SNP chief Peter Murrell (recently jailed).

stories.title: SNP Condemns Reform's 'Despicable' Bid to Ban Gaelic in Election Material
stories.summary: The SNP condemned Reform UK's amendment to the Representation of the People Bill as 'anti-Scottish,' saying it would effectively criminalise the use of Gaelic and Scots language in election materials.

stories.title: Yes Scotland Statement on Peter Murrell Dismissed as 'Weasel Words'
stories.summary: A statement released by former Yes Scotland boss Blair Jenkins regarding Peter Murrell — who was recently jailed for five years — was dismissed as 'weasel words' by lawyer Aamer Anwar.

stories.title: Cyber Security Responsibility Transferred from Cabinet Office to DSIT
stories.summary: The government announced that responsibility for Cyber Security policy has transferred from the Cabinet Office to the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) to streamline delivery and strengthen national priorities.

stories.title: Sixth Arrest Made in Election Fraud Investigation
stories.summary: A 25-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of conspiracy to defraud as part of an ongoing election fraud investigation. This is the sixth arrest in the case.

stories.title: Lib Dems Push for Voting System Change Before Next Election
stories.summary: Ed Davey urged incoming PM Andy Burnham to reform the UK's voting system before the next general election, calling it a key democratic commitment.

stories.title: Welsh Government Loses Budget Vote as Labour Refuses to Back Plaid Cymru
stories.summary: The Welsh Government suffered a budget vote defeat after Labour refused to support Plaid Cymru's spending plans. Plaid needed support from another party but could not broker a deal.

stories.title: Lord Barwell Loses Conservative Party Whip After Criticising Leadership
stories.summary: Former Tory minister Lord Gavin Barwell lost the Conservative party whip after speaking out against the party leadership. He said the decision shows 'intolerance of criticism' under Badenoch.

stories.title: House of Lords Debates Taxation (Energy and Vehicles) Bill
stories.summary: All stages of the Taxation (Energy and Vehicles) Bill were debated in the House of Lords. The debate covered energy and vehicle taxation reform as part of broader government economic policy.

stories.title: Tories Deny Purge as Multiple Peers Push Back Against Badenoch's Direction
stories.summary: The Conservative Party denied orchestrating a purge of centrist members, even as multiple figures lost or faced pressure over their party positions. The row follows Badenoch's ideological shift against net zero and centrist positions.
